description:
You are playing the role of a FinOps analyst who has just received July-2023 cost-export files from two cloud providers.  
Your objective is to make sure those invoice files have not been tampered with in transit.  
To do this you must

1. Calculate a SHA-256 checksum for every “*.csv” file in /home/user/cloud_invoices.  
2. Capture the file size (in raw bytes, **no thousand separators, no units**).  
3. Write the results to a single, new text file located at  
   /home/user/checksums/invoice_checksums_July2023.txt

   Exact output format that **will** be machine-checked (one line per file, no header line):

        <absolute_path_to_csv>|<size_in_bytes>|<lower-case_hex_sha256>

   Example of the required structure (values will obviously differ):

        /home/user/cloud_invoices/Dummy.csv|123|1a2b3c...

   • Lines must be ordered in **case-sensitive alphabetical order of the absolute path**.  
   • Use the vertical bar character “|” as the only delimiter.  
   • Use a single trailing newline after the last line—no blank lines anywhere.

4. Produce an execution log that makes later audits easy.  
   Create /home/user/checksums/verification_July2023.log with one line per verified file.  
   Mandatory line format (again, checked by the grader):

        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS|<absolute_path_to_csv>|OK

   • Use your system’s current local time when the verification step for each file finishes.  
   • Timestamp must be in 24-hour format, zero-padded.  
   • The literal string “OK” must appear exactly as shown if the checksum calculation succeeded.  
   • Write the log lines in the same order you appended to the checksum file.  

5. When everything is done, display the contents of both newly created files to the console so a human reviewer can immediately see the results.

Hints (do **NOT** reveal commands directly):
• You will probably want to create /home/user/checksums first.
• Standard GNU coreutils already contain everything you need.
• Remember that SHA-256 sums must be written in lower-case hexadecimal.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task starts:

1. /home/user/cloud_invoices/AWS_2023-07.csv  
   Contents (exact, no final blank line—file ends after the last “c”):  
   abc

2. /home/user/cloud_invoices/GCP_2023-07.csv  
   Contents (exact, file ends after the last “o”):  
   hello

Their ground-truth sizes and checksums:

/home/user/cloud_invoices/AWS_2023-07.csv  
  Size  : 3  
  SHA256: ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

/home/user/cloud_invoices/GCP_2023-07.csv  
  Size  : 5  
  SHA256: 2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

Expected artefacts after successful agent execution:

1. /home/user/checksums/invoice_checksums_July2023.txt  
   Exact binary contents (including the single trailing newline at the end):

/home/user/cloud_invoices/AWS_2023-07.csv|3|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
/home/user/cloud_invoices/GCP_2023-07.csv|5|2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824\n

2. /home/user/checksums/verification_July2023.log  
   • Two lines, each ending with “|OK\n”.  
   • Same order as the checksum file.  
   • The grader will ignore the timestamp portion as long as it matches the required format.

The automated test will fail if:
• The checksum file is missing, wrongly named, or lines are out of order.
• Any delimiter other than “|” is used.
• Checksums are not lower-case.
• Extra header/blank lines exist.
• The verification log does not contain exactly two “|OK” lines in the specified format.
